text.title: Fine-tuning (LoRA) — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `fine-tuning-lora` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Target ALL linear layers (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj), not just attention.

rationale.source: Hu et al. 2024 LoRA follow-up; Unsloth docs

rule.rationale: Attention-only targeting loses 5-15 points on instruction-following benchmarks.

rule.statement: Start r=16, alpha=32 (alpha=2*r). Bump to r=32/64 for harder tasks; never set alpha<r.

rationale.source: Dettmers et al. 2023; HF PEFT defaults

rule.rationale: alpha=2r is empirically stable; alpha<r under-scales and slows learning.

rule.statement: Use QLoRA when VRAM budget < 1.5x model size; LoRA otherwise.
rule.rationale: QLoRA gives 90-95% LoRA quality at 25-40% VRAM.

rule.statement: Mix 20-30% general-domain data with task data.
rule.rationale: Pure-task LoRA loses general capabilities; 20-30% preserves them.

rule.statement: lr=2e-4 for r<=16; lr=1e-4 for r>=32. Halve lr when training >3 epochs.
rule.rationale: Higher-rank adapters absorb more signal per step and instabilise at default lr.

rule.statement: Decide adapter-keep vs merge-into-base BEFORE training.
rule.rationale: Merging is one-way; keeping adapters lets one base model serve many tasks via swap.
